{{Short description|Japanese archaeological site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kunidominakamura Kofun | native_name =国富中村古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[grave goods]] included one [[bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[Sue ware]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[Tabushi Station]] on the [[Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/54 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Japanese archaeological site}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kunidominakamura Kofun | native_name =国富中村古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[grave goods]] included one [[bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[Sue ware]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[Tabushi Station]] on the [[Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

{{Short description|Japanese archaeological site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Kunidominakamura Kofun | native_name =国富中村古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition[[Category:addition]], there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[grave goods]] included one [[bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition[[Category:addition]], three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[Sue pottery]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[Tabushi Station]] on the [[Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/54 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun]][[en:Kunidominakamura Kofun]][[ja:国富中村古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109366133]] Wikidata name: Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese archaeological site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kunidominakamura Kofun | native_name =国富中村古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.42755|N|132.80079|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|qid=Q825166}} | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included one [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tabushi Station|Tabushi Station]] on the [[:en:Kita-Matsue Line|Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/54 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun]][[en:Kunidominakamura Kofun]][[ja:国富中村古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109366133]] Wikidata name: Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese archaeological site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kunidominakamura Kofun | native_name =国富中村古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.42755|N|132.80079|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included one [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tabushi Station|Tabushi Station]] on the [[:en:Kita-Matsue Line|Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included one [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tabushi Station|Tabushi Station]] on the [[:en:Kita-Matsue Line|Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun]][[en:Kunidominakamura Kofun]][[ja:国富中村古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109366133]] Wikidata name: Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese archaeological site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kunidominakamura Kofun | native_name =国富中村古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.42755|N|132.80079|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included one [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tabushi Station|Tabushi Station]] on the [[:en:Kita-Matsue Line|Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/54 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included one [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tabushi Station|Tabushi Station]] on the [[:en:Kita-Matsue Line|Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/54 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included one [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from [[:en:Tabushi Station|Tabushi Station]] on the [[:en:Kita-Matsue Line|Kita-Matsue Line]] of the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]].

The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}}. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included one {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tabushi Station|en|Tabushi Station|WD=Q11504488|lt=Tabushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kita-Matsue Line|en|Kita-Matsue Line|WD=Q11352976|lt=Kita-Matsue Line}} of the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|WD=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway}}.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/54 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Japanese archaeological site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kunidominakamura Kofun | native_name =国富中村古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.42755|N|132.80079|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}}. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included one {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tabushi Station|en|Tabushi Station|WD=Q11504488|lt=Tabushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kita-Matsue Line|en|Kita-Matsue Line|WD=Q11352976|lt=Kita-Matsue Line}} of the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|WD=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway}}.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/54 Shimane Prefecture Cultural Properties database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Japanese archaeological site}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kunidominakamura Kofun | native_name =国富中村古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kunidomi-Nakamura Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kunidominakamura Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.42755|N|132.80079|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|39|N|132|48|03|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included one {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tabushi Station|en|Tabushi Station|WD=Q11504488|lt=Tabushi Station|1=Tabushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kita-Matsue Line|en|Kita-Matsue Line|WD=Q11352976|lt=Kita-Matsue Line|1=Kita-Matsue Line}} of the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|WD=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway|1=Ichibata Electric Railway}}.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tabushi Station|en|Tabushi Station|WD=Q11504488|lt=Tabushi Station|1=Tabushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kita-Matsue Line|en|Kita-Matsue Line|WD=Q11352976|lt=Kita-Matsue Line|1=Kita-Matsue Line}} of the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|WD=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway|1=Ichibata Electric Railway}}.

The {{nihongo|'''Kunidominakamura Kofun'''|国富中村古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kunidomari neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|qid=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2013.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/240615|title=国富中村古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== Mount Kitayama is a massif that stretches from east to west on the north side of the Izumo Plain. At the western end of this massif is [[Hinomisaki Shrine]], and at the eastern end is Mt. Tabushi, which is 462 meters above sea level. The Kunidominakamura Kofun is located in eastern Shimane Prefecture at the southern foot of Mount Tabushi. The tumulus was leveled in road construction in 2002 and its existence only became known when construction workers found the tunnel-stye stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The size of the burial chamber and evidence that it had never been robbed drew immediate archaeological attention. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Excavation]]s have determined that the ''kofun'' was originally an {{nihongo|''[[Round Kofun|enpun]]''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 30 meters. The chamber itself was over 9.3 meters in length and consisted of a burial chamber itself and an antechamber. The burial chamber is 3.3 meters long, 1.9 meters wide and 2 meters high, the antechamber is 2.5 meters long, 1.2 to 1.5 meters wide and 1.8 meters high, and the corridor is 1.8 meters long. The entry to the stone chamber is blocked with natural stones, and the blocking stone completely preserved the conditions at the time of burial.
Inside the burial chamber was house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The cover stone is broken into three large pieces, and it is believed that the damage was done intentionally because the marks of hammer blows can be seen on the cover stone. In addition, there is a box-shaped sarcophagus without a lid in the antechamber, and it is thought that burial was performed twice, first in a house-shaped sarcophagus, then in the box-shaped sarcophagus. The chamber has not been completely excavated for preservation purposes, but {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included one {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, one gold ring, one silver ring, three glass beads, four bronze bells, three decorative iron swords, and three iron knives. In addition, three sets of iron arrowheads, three sets of horse harnesses and 61 pieces of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} were found. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tumulus was built between the end of the 6th century and the beginning of the 7th century. As a result of stratigraphic excavation of the burial chamber, it was determined that two iron swords were initially arranged by leaning them against the wall of the burial chamber, while other have been ritually broken. The intentional damage to the sarcophagus also points to some ritual destruction.
The Kunidominakamura Kofun is 1.1 kilometers, or approximately 12 minutes on foot from {{ill|Tabushi Station|en|Tabushi Station|qid=Q11504488|lt=Tabushi Station|1=Tabushi Station}} on the {{ill|Kita-Matsue Line|en|Kita-Matsue Line|qid=Q11352976|lt=Kita-Matsue Line|1=Kita-Matsue Line}} of the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|qid=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway|1=Ichibata Electric Railway}}.
